description.p: Works with password folders that use the standard pass layout, so you can keep using the folders you already have.

ul.li: Open one or more password folders, then search, filter, and edit what is inside
Create passwords and quickly copy passwords, usernames, and one-time login codes
Edit entries with simple fields or as plain text
Find weak passwords and spot repeated usernames, email addresses, and website links
Add a password folder you already have, create a new one, or restore one from Git
Import passwords from other apps on supported Linux systems
Choose which keys can unlock a folder, including password-protected keys, security keys, and OpenPGP smartcards
Create new keys or import keys you already have
Use Git to sync password folders, choose where they sync, and sign your changes
Inspect change history to see what changed and whether a commit was verified
For extra-sensitive folders, require more than one key before they open

screenshot.caption: Inspect change history and verify commits

ul.li: On the recipients page, you can now choose which folder-specific .gpg-id file you want to edit.
When you change recipients, Keycord now changes the selected folder's .gpg-id file instead of always changing only the top-level file.
When a store has many recipients, Keycord now prefers a usable local private key instead of trying the wrong person's key first.
GNOME search now shows less metadata and no longer looks inside secret fields.
Sensitive command output is now hidden more often in failed commands and logs.
Secrets are now handled more safely with better file writes, stronger process hardening, and stricter recipient checks.
The entry editor now has a clear save button.
The entry list now has quick add and clear-search actions at the bottom.
Apply buttons now stay hidden until all needed information is filled in.
Keyboard navigation now works better, especially with the arrow keys.
New history tool: read old changes and check whether a Git commit was verified.
You can now search or filter every page, so settings and options are easier to find.
The entry editor now shows the password check tool.
Removed the FIDO2 backed password stores feature from the flatpak app. Contact me if you want it back.
Added FIDO2-protected OpenPGP keys.
Fixed OTP bug where valid secrets were incorrectly invalidated.
Added FIDO2 guides and progress indicators.
Change the default to use the filename as the username.
Only enable FIDO2 features if the integrated backend is used.
Add documentation
Add search profider
You can now open pass files in a new window
Add OpenPGP hardware key support for private keys
Expand the password editor with cleanup actions
You can now use the field values as variables in search
Add the option to copy the logs
Fix optional Git synchronization
Changed the app icon to a keycord
Show disabled options instead of hiding them
Add tools page
Add advanced search
Add optional GPG synchronization
You can now manage Git remotes

description.p: You can now generate new private keys.
